concepts.title: Battery
concepts.conceptBody:
Cells

For an electrochemical cell to be used as a commercial cell. It must

1. Be compact, light and rugged.

2. Voltage should not drop during use.

Battery 

A number of cells connected in series forms a battery. There are three types of cells:

1. Primary cells 

In these the redox reactions occur only once and cannot be used again.

Example: dry cells, mercury cells.

2. Secondary cells 

These can be recharged by passing current and can be used again and again.

Example: Lead storage battery, Ni-Cd storage cell.

3. Fuel cell 

Energy produced by combustion of fuels like ${{\text{H}}_{2}},\text{CO},\text{C}{{\text{H}}_{4}}$ can be directly converted to electric energy.

concepts.title: Dry cells
concepts.conceptBody:
Primary cells

Dry cells 

It is the compact form of the Leclanche cell.

Anode - Cylindrical Zn container.

Cathode - Central Graphite rod.

The space in between is filled with $\text{N}{{\text{H}}_{4}}\text{Cl}$ and $\text{ZnC}{{\text{l}}_{2}}$. The graphite rod is surrounded by $\text{Mn}{{\text{O}}_{2}}$ and carbon.

Reactions: 

Anode $\text{Z}{{\text{n}}_{(\text{s})}}\to \text{Zn}_{(\text{aq})}^{2+}+2{{\text{e}}^{-}}$

Cathode: $2\text{Mn}{{\text{O}}_{2(~\text{s})}}+2\text{NH}{{_{4}^{+}}_{(\text{aq})}}+2{{\text{e}}^{-}}\to \text{M}{{\text{n}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{3(~\text{s})}}+2\text{N}{{\text{H}}_{3}}+{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{O}$

Voltage: 1.25 to $1.5~\text{V}$

It does not have a long life because the acidic $\text{N}{{\text{H}}_{4}}\text{Cl}$ corrodes the Zinc container.

concepts.title: Lead storage battery
concepts.conceptBody:
Secondary Cell

Lead storage battery 

Anode - Lead

Cathode - $\text{Pb}{{\text{O}}_{2}}$

The electrodes are arranged alternately, separated by thin wooden or fibre glass sheet.

Electrolyte - dilute ${{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{4}}$.

Electron reaction (during discharging)

Anode: $\text{P}{{\text{b}}_{(\text{s})}}+\text{SO}_{4}^{2-}\to \text{PbS}{{\text{O}}_{4}}+2{{\text{e}}^{-}}$

Cathode: $\text{Pb}{{\text{O}}_{2}}+\text{SO}_{4}^{2-}+4{{\text{H}}^{+}}+2{{\text{e}}^{-}}\to \text{PbS}{{\text{O}}_{4}}+{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{O}$

Over all: $\text{P}{{\text{b}}_{(\text{s})}}+\text{Pb}{{\text{O}}_{2}}+4{{\text{H}}^{+}}+2\text{SO}_{4}^{2-}\to 2\text{PbS}{{\text{O}}_{4}}+2{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{O}$

During charging, reverse reactions take place

$\text{PbS}{{\text{O}}_{4}}+2{{\text{e}}^{-}}\to \text{P}{{\text{b}}_{(\text{s})}}+\text{SO}_{4}^{2-}$

$\text{PbS}{{\text{O}}_{4}}+2{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{O}\to \text{Pb}{{\text{O}}_{2}}+\text{SO}_{4}^{2-}+4{{\text{H}}^{+}}+2{{\text{e}}^{-}}$

Over all \(2 \mathrm{PbSO}_4+2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Pb}_{(\mathrm{s})}+\mathrm{PbO}_{2(\mathrm{~s})}+4 \mathrm{H}_{(\mathrm{aq})}^{+}+2 \mathrm{SO}_{4(\mathrm{aq})}^{2-}\)

concepts.title: Nickel cadmium storage cell
concepts.conceptBody:
Nickel Cadmium storage cell
Used mainly in calculators
Anode - Cadmium
Reactions
Anode: $\text{C}{{\text{d}}_{(\text{s})}}+2\text{OH}_{(\text{aq})}^{-}\to \text{Cd}{{(\text{OH})}_{2}}+2{{\text{e}}^{-}}$
Cathode: $\text{Ni}{{\text{O}}_{2}}+2{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{O}+2{{\text{e}}^{-}}\to \text{Ni}{{(\text{OH})}_{2}}+2\text{O}{{\text{H}}^{-}}$
can be recharged.

concepts.title: Mercury Cell
concepts.conceptBody:
Mercury Cell 

Used in small electric devices like hearing aids and watches.

Anode - Zinc container

Cathode - Carbon rod

Electrolyte - moist $\text{HgO}$ mixed with $\text{KOH}$

Reactions: 

Anode$:\text{Z}{{\text{n}}_{(\text{s})}}+2\text{O}{{\text{H}}^{-}}\to \text{Zn}{{\text{O}}_{(\text{s})}}+{{\text{H}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{(\text{I})}}+2\text{e}$

Cathode: $\text{HgO}(\text{s})+{{\text{H}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{(\text{I})}}+2\text{e}\to \text{H}{{\text{g}}_{(\text{I})}}+2\text{O}{{\text{H}}^{-}}$

Overall reaction: $\text{Z}{{\text{n}}_{(\text{s})}}+\text{Hg}{{\text{O}}_{(\text{s})}}\to \text{Zn}{{\text{O}}_{(\text{s})}}+\text{H}{{\text{g}}_{(\text{I})}}$

The cell shows a constant potential of $1.35~\text{V}$ throughout as it does not involve any ion whose concentration changes.

concepts.title: H2-O2 fuel cell
concepts.conceptBody:
Fuel cell

These are devices which convert the energy produced during the combustion of fuels like ${{\text{H}}_{2}},\text{CO}$ and $\text{C}{{\text{H}}_{4}}$ directly into electrical energy. The most successful fuel cell is the ${{\text{H}}_{2}}-{{\text{O}}_{2}}$ fuel cell. It was used in the Apollo space programme and the water produced used as drinking water for the astronauts.

Working 

${{\text{H}}_{2}}$ and ${{\text{O}}_{2}}$ are bubbled through a porous carbon rod into concentrated $\text{NaOH}$. Catalysts are present in the electrodes.

Advantages 

(1) More efficient than conventional cells because the energy of the fuel is converted directly

into electric energy. Efficiency = 60 – 70%

(2) They are free from pollution.
